steps.explanation: Square both sides to eliminate the square root: \(\frac{C^{2}V^{4}}{P^{2}} = 18P\). Multiply both sides by \(P^{2}\): \(C^{2}V^{4} = 18P^{3}\), so \(P^{3} = \frac{C^{2}V^{4}}{18}\).
Take the cube root of both sides: \(P = \sqrt[3]{\frac{C^{2}V^{4}}{18}}\).

Evaluating all choices:
  • Choice A: \(P = \frac{CV^{2}}{\sqrt{18P}}\) still contains \(P\) on the right side, so it is not an explicit expression for \(P\). Incorrect.
  • Choice B: \(P = \frac{CV^{2}}{\sqrt{18}}\). Cubing gives \(P^{3} = \frac{C^{3}V^{6}}{18\sqrt{18}}\), which does not match \(P^{3} = \frac{C^{2}V^{4}}{18}\). Incorrect.
  • Choice C: The "3" denotes a cube root, so \(P = \sqrt[3]{\frac{C^{2}V^{4}}{18}}\). Cubing gives \(P^{3} = \frac{C^{2}V^{4}}{18}\), which matches exactly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • Choice D: \(P = \sqrt[3]{\frac{18}{C^{2}V^{4}}}\). Cubing gives \(P^{3} = \frac{18}{C^{2}V^{4}}\), the reciprocal of what is needed. Incorrect.
